projectDescription: A PS5 payload that exploits a hypervisor vulnerability to boot Linux on PS5 Phat consoles running firmware 3.xx or 4.xx, unlocking full hardware capabilities including an 8-core CPU, AMD GPU, 4K HDMI output, and M.2 SSD support.
keyFeatures: Hypervisor (HV) exploit to escape PS5 GameOS VM and gain bare-metal Linux access
Support for PS5 Phat on firmware 3.xx and 4.xx
Full AMD GPU support with amdgpu driver (2.23 GHz, 4K60 HDMI)
8-core / 16-thread CPU at 3.5 GHz with boost control
M.2 SSD support as dedicated Linux storage partition
Configurable kernel cmdline and VRAM size via USB FAT32 partition files
IOMMU-based physical memory writes used to patch VMCBs
Kernel shellcode injection hooking ACPI wakeup path for seamless Linux boot on resume
publicApiSurface: hv_defeat() — orchestrates all hypervisor defeat stages
fetch_linux(struct linux_info *info) — loads bzImage, initrd, cmdline, and vram config from USB
stage1_tmr_relax() — relaxes TMR (timer) restrictions in the HV
stage2_find_vmcbs() — discovers VMCB physical addresses per CPU core
stage3_patch_vmcbs() — patches VMCBs via IOMMU to disable HV enforcement
stage4_force_vmcb_reload() — triggers VMCB reload via vmmcall on each core
stage5_remove_xotext() — removes execute-only page protection from kernel text
stage6_kernel_pmap_invalidate_all() — invalidates kernel page mappings via pipe trick
stage7_install_kexec() — installs kexec shellcode into kernel code cave
iommu_write8_pa(uint64_t pa, uint64_t val) — writes 8 bytes to a physical address via IOMMU
gpu_init() — initializes GPU context for physical memory read/write
offset_list struct — per-firmware kernel/HV symbol offsets for 3.00–4.51
